ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-17-2025 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-03-2025 Continued To Dec-17-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-10-2025 Continued To Dec-03-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-18-2025 Continued To Nov-10-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Gilbert Jay for Petitioner and Administrator Robert Lee.

The Court addressed examiner's concerns regarding the estate, including appraisal of U.S. savings bonds and disposition of a vehicle. Mr. Jay explained that the savings bonds had been submitted directly to the Treasury and deposited into the estate account, making it difficult to determine the value of each bond for inventory and appraisal purposes. He noted that the assigned probate referee was no longer serving, and counsel sought guidance on assigning a new referee. The Court advised that a local form could be submitted ex parte to request appointment of a new referee.

Mr. Jay further reported that the decedent's Mustang had been sold during the pandemic for $5,000, which was $1,000 above the appraised value. Counsel requested the Court consider accepting a waiver of accounting from the beneficiary and allow the matter to close without further amendment. The Court emphasized that while the matter had become burdensome, it must be concluded properly. The Court directed that counsel coordinate with the examiner to resolve outstanding issues and obtain a waiver of accounting from the beneficiary, if that was the chosen course.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to November 10, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Dec-12-2022 Continued To May-15-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nicholas Jay, via Zoom, for Robert G. Lee, Personal Representative.

Counsel reports of a pending tax issue and pending petition for guardianship in Los Angeles County as the reason for the delay in filing a petition on this matter. The court and counsel discuss the pending inventory and appraisal and whether counsel might be able to obtain waivers on account. The court advises counsel that it is inclined to allow a continuance but that the court expects the filing of a petition for final distribution or petition for approval of status of administration prior to the next calling.

The Status Hearing is continued May-15-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Counsel is instructed to file either of the petitions mentioned above by May-08-2023.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-12-2022 Continued To Dec-12-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nicholas K. Jay, via Zoom, for Robert G. Lee, Administrator.

The court points to the partial inventory and appraisal and asks Mr. Jay for an update. Mr. Jay reports on the status of this matter and particularly of the delays caused by an IRS issue. Mr. Jay requests a continuance to prepare a petition. The court advises counsel that it intends to grant his request for a continuance, but the court is expecting a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration to be filed prior to the next hearing date or an appearance will be required.

The matter is continued to Dec-12-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.
